_comment: Phase 53 parity fixture for the memory extractor. The Phase 53 port (memory-extractor as a bundled extension on ctx.llm + ctx.memory) MUST produce the same set of memory facts when fed this conversation as the legacy host-side extractor does. Tolerance: facts must match by `category` + `content` substring (whitespace-insensitive); ordering may differ.

run_complete_event.finalContent: Got it — I'll remember you prefer Bun over Node and you're working on EZCorp.

recent_messages.content: By the way, my name is Alex Chen and I'm the lead engineer on EZCorp. I prefer Bun over Node.js for everything.
Got it. I'll remember those preferences for future conversations.
Also, the project uses PGlite locally and Postgres in prod. We deploy via single-container Docker.
Noted — single-container Docker, PGlite dev → Postgres prod migration.

_phase_53_notes: The bundled memory-extractor MUST stamp `provenance.injectionEligible: false` on each created memory (Phase 51.2 invariant), differing from the legacy extractor's default of true. Tests asserting parity should use `content_substring` matches rather than exact strings — the extractor's word choices may vary across LLM calls.
